forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8180'
+title: 'RepoMetadata: fall back to text_config for VLM configs (e.g. Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4)'
+---
+## What
+
+Vision-language model repos (e.g. `Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`, and the broader Qwen-VL family) publish a `config.json` where the transformer sizing fields â€” `num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size` â€” are nested under a `text_config` object instead of appearing at the top level (top level instead holds `vision_config` and VLM-specific keys). `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift` only reads these fields from the top level of `RepoConfig`, so every VLM repo fails profile resolution with `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")` before inference ever starts â€” a 100% failure, not intermittent flakiness.
+
+Fix `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift`:
+
+1. Add a nested `TextConfig: Decodable` struct to the private `RepoConfig` struct (around line 193), decoding the same five optional fields (`num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size`) via the same snake_case `CodingKeys` mapping.
+2. Add a `let textConfig: TextConfig?` field to `RepoConfig`, keyed `"text_config"` in its `CodingKeys`.
+3. In `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` (around lines 129-150), change every read of `config.numHiddenLayers`, `config.numAttentionHeads`, `config.numKeyValueHeads`, `config.headDim`, `config.hiddenSize` to fall back to the matching `config.textConfig?.*` field when the top-level value is `nil` (e.g. `config.numHiddenLayers ?? config.textConfig?.numHiddenLayers`). Top-level values still win when present â€” this is a fallback, not a replacement.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] A `config.json` shaped like a VLM's (all five sizing fields absent at top level, present only under a `text_config` object) parses successfully into a `RepoMetadata` instead of throwing `RepoMetadataError.metadataUnavailable`.
+- [ ] A `config.json` with fields at both top level and under `text_config` prefers the top-level values (fallback only applies when top-level is absent).
+- [ ] All existing `RepoMetadataTests` cases still pass unchanged (top-level-only configs are unaffected).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Add `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/RepoMetadataTests.swift` test `textConfigFallback` (or similar `@Test` name): canned `config.json` with `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads`/`num_key_value_heads`/`head_dim`/`hidden_size` nested under `"text_config"` and absent at top level (mirroring a Qwen-VL-style config with a sibling `vision_config` block) â€” asserts `RepoMetadataReader.metadata(for:)` succeeds and returns the expected values, following the pattern of `happyPathMetadata`.
+- [ ] Add a second test asserting top-level fields take precedence over `text_config` fields when both are present.
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter RepoMetadataTests` â€” expect all tests, including the two new ones, to pass.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,31 +0,0 @@
----

-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: '8180'
-title: 'RepoMetadata: fall back to text_config for VLM configs (e.g. Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4)'
----
-## What
-
-Vision-language model repos (e.g. `Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`, and the broader Qwen-VL family) publish a `config.json` where the transformer sizing fields â€” `num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size` â€” are nested under a `text_config` object instead of appearing at the top level (top level instead holds `vision_config` and VLM-specific keys). `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift` only reads these fields from the top level of `RepoConfig`, so every VLM repo fails profile resolution with `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")` before inference ever starts â€” a 100% failure, not intermittent flakiness.
-
-Fix `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift`:
-
-1. Add a nested `TextConfig: Decodable` struct to the private `RepoConfig` struct (around line 193), decoding the same five optional fields (`num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size`) via the same snake_case `CodingKeys` mapping.
-2. Add a `let textConfig: TextConfig?` field to `RepoConfig`, keyed `"text_config"` in its `CodingKeys`.
-3. In `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` (around lines 129-150), change every read of `config.numHiddenLayers`, `config.numAttentionHeads`, `config.numKeyValueHeads`, `config.headDim`, `config.hiddenSize` to fall back to the matching `config.textConfig?.*` field when the top-level value is `nil` (e.g. `config.numHiddenLayers ?? config.textConfig?.numHiddenLayers`). Top-level values still win when present â€” this is a fallback, not a replacement.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] A `config.json` shaped like a VLM's (all five sizing fields absent at top level, present only under a `text_config` object) parses successfully into a `RepoMetadata` instead of throwing `RepoMetadataError.metadataUnavailable`.
-- [ ] A `config.json` with fields at both top level and under `text_config` prefers the top-level values (fallback only applies when top-level is absent).
-- [ ] All existing `RepoMetadataTests` cases still pass unchanged (top-level-only configs are unaffected).
-
-## Tests
-
-- [ ] Add `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/RepoMetadataTests.swift` test `textConfigFallback` (or similar `@Test` name): canned `config.json` with `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads`/`num_key_value_heads`/`head_dim`/`hidden_size` nested under `"text_config"` and absent at top level (mirroring a Qwen-VL-style config with a sibling `vision_config` block) â€” asserts `RepoMetadataReader.metadata(for:)` succeeds and returns the expected values, following the pattern of `happyPathMetadata`.
-- [ ] Add a second test asserting top-level fields take precedence over `text_config` fields when both are present.
-- [ ] Run `swift test --filter RepoMetadataTests` â€” expect all tests, including the two new ones, to pass.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,7 +7,11 @@
 ---
 ## What

-Vision-language model repos (e.g. `Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`, and the broader Qwen-VL family) publish a `config.json` where the transformer sizing fields â€” `num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size` â€” are nested under a `text_config` object instead of appearing at the top level (top level instead holds `vision_config` and VLM-specific keys). `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift` only reads these fields from the top level of `RepoConfig`, so every VLM repo fails profile resolution with `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")` before inference ever starts â€” a 100% failure, not intermittent flakiness.
+Vision-language model repos (e.g. `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`, and the broader Qwen-VL family) publish a `config.json` where the transformer sizing fields â€” `num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size` â€” are nested under a `text_config` object instead of appearing at the top level (top level instead holds `vision_config` and VLM-specific keys). `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift` only reads these fields from the top level of `RepoConfig`, so every VLM repo fails profile resolution with `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")` before inference ever starts â€” a 100% failure, not intermittent flakiness.
+
+Confirmed against the real repo: `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4/resolve/main/config.json` has no `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads` at its top level. They live at `text_config.num_hidden_layers: 24`, `text_config.num_attention_heads: 8`, `text_config.num_key_value_heads: 2`, `text_config.head_dim: 256`, `text_config.hidden_size: 2048`. Its sibling `vision_config` uses distinct field names (`depth`, `num_heads`) rather than colliding with the same keys, so for this repo a top-level-then-`text_config` fallback is unambiguous.
+
+**Known limitation, explicitly out of scope for this task:** `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`'s `text_config` also declares a hybrid attention architecture (`layer_types` alternates `linear_attention`/`full_attention` every 4th layer via `full_attention_interval: 4`, with separate `linear_num_key_heads`/`linear_key_head_dim` for the linear-attention layers). Once this task lands, profile resolution will succeed for this repo, but `Footprint`'s uniform-GQA KV-cache formula will still overestimate cache size â€” it assumes every layer grows a cache the same way, when only the `full_attention` layers (6 of 24 here) actually do. That correctness gap is a separate follow-up, not part of this fix.

 Fix `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift`:

@@ -17,14 +21,14 @@

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] A `config.json` shaped like a VLM's (all five sizing fields absent at top level, present only under a `text_config` object) parses successfully into a `RepoMetadata` instead of throwing `RepoMetadataError.metadataUnavailable`.
+- [ ] The real `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4` `config.json` shape (sizing fields absent at top level, present only under `text_config`, alongside a sibling `vision_config`) parses successfully into a `RepoMetadata` instead of throwing `RepoMetadataError.metadataUnavailable`.
 - [ ] A `config.json` with fields at both top level and under `text_config` prefers the top-level values (fallback only applies when top-level is absent).
 - [ ] All existing `RepoMetadataTests` cases still pass unchanged (top-level-only configs are unaffected).

 ## Tests

-- [ ] Add `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/RepoMetadataTests.swift` test `textConfigFallback` (or similar `@Test` name): canned `config.json` with `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads`/`num_key_value_heads`/`head_dim`/`hidden_size` nested under `"text_config"` and absent at top level (mirroring a Qwen-VL-style config with a sibling `vision_config` block) â€” asserts `RepoMetadataReader.metadata(for:)` succeeds and returns the expected values, following the pattern of `happyPathMetadata`.
-- [ ] Add a second test asserting top-level fields take precedence over `text_config` fields when both are present.
+- [ ] Add `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/RepoMetadataTests.swift` test `qwenVLTextConfigFallback` (or similar `@Test` name) using the **actual fetched** `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4` `config.json` as the canned fixture (verbatim `text_config` and `vision_config` blocks, as captured above) â€” asserts `RepoMetadataReader.metadata(for:)` succeeds and returns `numHiddenLayers == 24`, `numAttentionHeads == 8`, `numKeyValueHeads == 2`, `headDim == 256`, `hiddenSize == 2048`, following the pattern of `happyPathMetadata`.
+- [ ] Add a second test asserting top-level fields take precedence over `text_config` fields when both are present (synthetic fixture is fine here â€” precedence isn't specific to this repo).
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter RepoMetadataTests` â€” expect all tests, including the two new ones, to pass.

 ## Workflow

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@

 Confirmed against the real repo: `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4/resolve/main/config.json` has no `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads` at its top level. They live at `text_config.num_hidden_layers: 24`, `text_config.num_attention_heads: 8`, `text_config.num_key_value_heads: 2`, `text_config.head_dim: 256`, `text_config.hidden_size: 2048`. Its sibling `vision_config` uses distinct field names (`depth`, `num_heads`) rather than colliding with the same keys, so for this repo a top-level-then-`text_config` fallback is unambiguous.

-**Known limitation, explicitly out of scope for this task:** `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`'s `text_config` also declares a hybrid attention architecture (`layer_types` alternates `linear_attention`/`full_attention` every 4th layer via `full_attention_interval: 4`, with separate `linear_num_key_heads`/`linear_key_head_dim` for the linear-attention layers). Once this task lands, profile resolution will succeed for this repo, but `Footprint`'s uniform-GQA KV-cache formula will still overestimate cache size â€” it assumes every layer grows a cache the same way, when only the `full_attention` layers (6 of 24 here) actually do. That correctness gap is a separate follow-up, not part of this fix.
+This same repo's `text_config` also declares a hybrid linear/full-attention architecture (`layer_types`, `full_attention_interval`) that affects KV-cache sizing correctness â€” that is tracked separately as ^2x1rv1q (blocked on this task) and is not part of this fix.

 Fix `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift`:

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,26 +9,29 @@

 Vision-language model repos (e.g. `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`, and the broader Qwen-VL family) publish a `config.json` where the transformer sizing fields â€” `num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size` â€” are nested under a `text_config` object instead of appearing at the top level (top level instead holds `vision_config` and VLM-specific keys). `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift` only reads these fields from the top level of `RepoConfig`, so every VLM repo fails profile resolution with `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")` before inference ever starts â€” a 100% failure, not intermittent flakiness.

-Confirmed against the real repo: `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4/resolve/main/config.json` has no `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads` at its top level. They live at `text_config.num_hidden_layers: 24`, `text_config.num_attention_heads: 8`, `text_config.num_key_value_heads: 2`, `text_config.head_dim: 256`, `text_config.hidden_size: 2048`. Its sibling `vision_config` uses distinct field names (`depth`, `num_heads`) rather than colliding with the same keys, so for this repo a top-level-then-`text_config` fallback is unambiguous.
+Confirmed against the real repo: `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4/resolve/main/config.json` has no `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads` at its top level. They live at `text_config.num_hidden_layers: 24`, `text_config.num_attention_heads: 8`, `text_config.num_key_value_heads: 2`, `text_config.head_dim: 256`, `text_config.hidden_size: 2048`. Its sibling `vision_config` uses distinct field names (`depth`, `num_heads`) rather than colliding with the same keys.

 This same repo's `text_config` also declares a hybrid linear/full-attention architecture (`layer_types`, `full_attention_interval`) that affects KV-cache sizing correctness â€” that is tracked separately as ^2x1rv1q (blocked on this task) and is not part of this fix.

+**Resolution rule â€” pick one coherent source, never mix fields across levels.** This mirrors HF transformers' own `get_text_config()` semantics: a composite config's language-model fields are read as a unit from one object. Per-field `??` merging across top level and `text_config` could stitch together fields from different stacks (e.g. a top-level projector-related `hidden_size` with `text_config`'s head counts) and silently size the KV cache wrong.
+
 Fix `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift`:

 1. Add a nested `TextConfig: Decodable` struct to the private `RepoConfig` struct (around line 193), decoding the same five optional fields (`num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size`) via the same snake_case `CodingKeys` mapping.
 2. Add a `let textConfig: TextConfig?` field to `RepoConfig`, keyed `"text_config"` in its `CodingKeys`.
-3. In `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` (around lines 129-150), change every read of `config.numHiddenLayers`, `config.numAttentionHeads`, `config.numKeyValueHeads`, `config.headDim`, `config.hiddenSize` to fall back to the matching `config.textConfig?.*` field when the top-level value is `nil` (e.g. `config.numHiddenLayers ?? config.textConfig?.numHiddenLayers`). Top-level values still win when present â€” this is a fallback, not a replacement.
+3. In `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` (around lines 129-150), select the sizing source as a whole: if the top-level config has both `num_hidden_layers` and `num_attention_heads`, read **all five** fields from the top level (current behavior, unchanged); otherwise, if `textConfig` has both required fields, read **all five** fields from `textConfig`; otherwise throw the existing `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")`. The existing `head_dim`/`hidden_size` presence check then applies to whichever source was selected.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

 - [ ] The real `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4` `config.json` shape (sizing fields absent at top level, present only under `text_config`, alongside a sibling `vision_config`) parses successfully into a `RepoMetadata` instead of throwing `RepoMetadataError.metadataUnavailable`.
-- [ ] A `config.json` with fields at both top level and under `text_config` prefers the top-level values (fallback only applies when top-level is absent).
+- [ ] A `config.json` whose top level has the two required fields is read entirely from the top level â€” `text_config` values are ignored even when present (no per-field mixing in either direction).
+- [ ] A `config.json` where neither the top level nor `text_config` has both required fields still throws `metadataUnavailable`.
 - [ ] All existing `RepoMetadataTests` cases still pass unchanged (top-level-only configs are unaffected).

 ## Tests

 - [ ] Add `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/RepoMetadataTests.swift` test `qwenVLTextConfigFallback` (or similar `@Test` name) using the **actual fetched** `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4` `config.json` as the canned fixture (verbatim `text_config` and `vision_config` blocks, as captured above) â€” asserts `RepoMetadataReader.metadata(for:)` succeeds and returns `numHiddenLayers == 24`, `numAttentionHeads == 8`, `numKeyValueHeads == 2`, `headDim == 256`, `hiddenSize == 2048`, following the pattern of `happyPathMetadata`.
-- [ ] Add a second test asserting top-level fields take precedence over `text_config` fields when both are present (synthetic fixture is fine here â€” precedence isn't specific to this repo).
+- [ ] Add a test asserting the coherent-source rule: a synthetic fixture with complete-but-different values at both top level and under `text_config` resolves every field from the top level (e.g. differing `num_key_value_heads` proves no per-field mixing).
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter RepoMetadataTests` â€” expect all tests, including the two new ones, to pass.

 ## Workflow

 Vision-language model repos (e.g. `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4`, and the broader Qwen-VL family) publish a `config.json` where the transformer sizing fields â€” `num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size` â€” are nested under a `text_config` object instead of appearing at the top level (top level instead holds `vision_config` and VLM-specific keys). `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` in `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift` only reads these fields from the top level of `RepoConfig`, so every VLM repo fails profile resolution with `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")` before inference ever starts â€” a 100% failure, not intermittent flakiness.

-Confirmed against the real repo: `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4/resolve/main/config.json` has no `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads` at its top level. They live at `text_config.num_hidden_layers: 24`, `text_config.num_attention_heads: 8`, `text_config.num_key_value_heads: 2`, `text_config.head_dim: 256`, `text_config.hidden_size: 2048`. Its sibling `vision_config` uses distinct field names (`depth`, `num_heads`) rather than colliding with the same keys.
+Confirmed against the real repo: `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4/resolve/main/config.json` has no `num_hidden_layers`/`num_attention_heads` at its top level. They live at `text_config.num_hidden_layers: 24`, `text_config.num_attention_heads: 8`, `text_config.num_key_value_heads: 2`, `text_config.head_dim: 256`, `text_config.hidden_size: 2048`. Its sibling `vision_config` uses distinct field names (`depth`, `num_heads`) rather than colliding with the same keys, so for this repo a top-level-then-`text_config` fallback is unambiguous.

 This same repo's `text_config` also declares a hybrid linear/full-attention architecture (`layer_types`, `full_attention_interval`) that affects KV-cache sizing correctness â€” that is tracked separately as ^2x1rv1q (blocked on this task) and is not part of this fix.

-**Resolution rule â€” pick one coherent source, never mix fields across levels.** This mirrors HF transformers' own `get_text_config()` semantics: a composite config's language-model fields are read as a unit from one object. Per-field `??` merging across top level and `text_config` could stitch together fields from different stacks (e.g. a top-level projector-related `hidden_size` with `text_config`'s head counts) and silently size the KV cache wrong.
-
 Fix `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Sizing/RepoMetadata.swift`:

 1. Add a nested `TextConfig: Decodable` struct to the private `RepoConfig` struct (around line 193), decoding the same five optional fields (`num_hidden_layers`, `num_attention_heads`, `num_key_value_heads`, `head_dim`, `hidden_size`) via the same snake_case `CodingKeys` mapping.
 2. Add a `let textConfig: TextConfig?` field to `RepoConfig`, keyed `"text_config"` in its `CodingKeys`.
-3. In `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` (around lines 129-150), select the sizing source as a whole: if the top-level config has both `num_hidden_layers` and `num_attention_heads`, read **all five** fields from the top level (current behavior, unchanged); otherwise, if `textConfig` has both required fields, read **all five** fields from `textConfig`; otherwise throw the existing `metadataUnavailable("config.json is missing num_hidden_layers or num_attention_heads")`. The existing `head_dim`/`hidden_size` presence check then applies to whichever source was selected.
+3. In `RepoMetadata.init(raw:)` (around lines 129-150), change every read of `config.numHiddenLayers`, `config.numAttentionHeads`, `config.numKeyValueHeads`, `config.headDim`, `config.hiddenSize` to fall back to the matching `config.textConfig?.*` field when the top-level value is `nil` (e.g. `config.numHiddenLayers ?? config.textConfig?.numHiddenLayers`). Top-level values still win when present â€” this is a fallback, not a replacement.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

 - [ ] The real `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4` `config.json` shape (sizing fields absent at top level, present only under `text_config`, alongside a sibling `vision_config`) parses successfully into a `RepoMetadata` instead of throwing `RepoMetadataError.metadataUnavailable`.
-- [ ] A `config.json` whose top level has the two required fields is read entirely from the top level â€” `text_config` values are ignored even when present (no per-field mixing in either direction).
-- [ ] A `config.json` where neither the top level nor `text_config` has both required fields still throws `metadataUnavailable`.
+- [ ] A `config.json` with fields at both top level and under `text_config` prefers the top-level values (fallback only applies when top-level is absent).
 - [ ] All existing `RepoMetadataTests` cases still pass unchanged (top-level-only configs are unaffected).

 ## Tests

 - [ ] Add `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/RepoMetadataTests.swift` test `qwenVLTextConfigFallback` (or similar `@Test` name) using the **actual fetched** `mlx-community/Qwen3.5-2B-mxfp4` `config.json` as the canned fixture (verbatim `text_config` and `vision_config` blocks, as captured above) â€” asserts `RepoMetadataReader.metadata(for:)` succeeds and returns `numHiddenLayers == 24`, `numAttentionHeads == 8`, `numKeyValueHeads == 2`, `headDim == 256`, `hiddenSize == 2048`, following the pattern of `happyPathMetadata`.
-- [ ] Add a test asserting the coherent-source rule: a synthetic fixture with complete-but-different values at both top level and under `text_config` resolves every field from the top level (e.g. differing `num_key_value_heads` proves no per-field mixing).
+- [ ] Add a second test asserting top-level fields take precedence over `text_config` fields when both are present (synthetic fixture is fine here â€” precedence isn't specific to this repo).
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter RepoMetadataTests` â€” expect all tests, including the two new ones, to pass.
